title: Token Truncation as Silent Failure
summary: When an LLM call hits the token cap, the response is truncated mid-JSON, surfacing as a parse error until output tolerance is fixed.
body: When an LLM call hits the token cap, the response gets truncated mid-JSON. This looks like a parse error ('invalid character "," after top-level value') until you fix the parser to tolerate trailing content. The real issue then surfaces: the `maxTokens` parameter was at default (1.5-2k) instead of the required 8192. Fix the parser tolerance first to expose the actual problem, then increase capacity. In RoleNext S1, nursing-resume searches failed on every call; the fix cascaded: first parse tolerance, then token cap adjustment to 8192, then real results.
